checklist.description: The preview endpoint returns a pack result (packCid, entries with compression details, compileStats) without creating any database record.
checklist.example: POST /diaries/:id/packs/preview calls fitCustomPackEntries and computePackCid, returns {packCid, packType, params, entries, compileStats} — contextPackRepository.createPack is never called.
checklist.name: preview_returns_result_without_persisting

checklist.description: The create endpoint saves the pack to the database, including per-entry records with compression level and token counts.
checklist.example: POST /diaries/:id/packs calls contextPackRepository.createPack() followed by contextPackRepository.addEntries() inside a transaction, then relationshipWriter.grantPackParent().
checklist.name: create_persists_pack_and_entries

checklist.description: The same entry selection (same diary, same entry content hashes, same ranks, same params) always produces the same packCid, independent of creation time or creator identity.
checklist.example: computePackCid is called with {diaryId, packType: 'custom', params, entries: [{cid: entryCidSnapshot, compressionLevel, rank}]} — createdAt and createdBy are not included in the CID computation.
checklist.name: deterministic_pack_cid

checklist.description: When a token budget is provided and the uncompressed total exceeds it, entries are compressed progressively to fit within the budget; entries that cannot fit even at maximum compression are excluded. The compile stats reflect the resulting token counts accurately.
checklist.example: fitCustomPackEntries escalates compressionLevel from 'full' to 'summary' to 'keywords' for lower-ranked entries, dropping them if the budget still cannot be met; compileStats.totalTokens reflects only the included entries.
checklist.name: token_budget_compression

checklist.description: Requests with duplicate entryId values or duplicate rank values are rejected with a validation error.
checklist.example: normalizeSelection() checks for duplicate entryId and duplicate rank, throwing a 400 validation-failed problem for each.
checklist.name: validation_rejects_duplicates

checklist.description: Requesting entries that do not exist in the specified diary returns an error identifying the missing entry IDs rather than silently omitting them.
checklist.example: loadSelectedEntries() compares the count of returned rows against the requested count; missing IDs are listed in the error message.
checklist.name: validation_rejects_missing_entries

checklist.description: New REST endpoints use the existing TypeBox type provider pattern (server.withTypeProvider<TypeBoxTypeProvider>()) and TypeBox schemas for request/response validation.
checklist.example: Routes defined on 'server' (from fastify.withTypeProvider<TypeBoxTypeProvider>()), request body validated with a TypeBox object schema, response schema uses Type.Ref(CustomPackResultSchema).
checklist.name: uses_fastify_typebox_provider

checklist.description: Pack identity is computed using the existing computePackCid utility from @moltnet/crypto-service rather than a custom hashing implementation.
checklist.example: import { computePackCid } from '@moltnet/crypto-service'; ... const packCid = computePackCid({ diaryId, packType: 'custom', params, entries }).
checklist.name: uses_compute_pack_cid

checklist.description: Token estimation and entry compression use the existing compress() and estimateTokens() utilities from @moltnet/context-distill.
checklist.example: import { compress, estimateTokens } from '@moltnet/context-distill'; tokens = estimateTokens(row.content); compiled = compress(entry, level).
checklist.name: uses_context_distill_for_compression

checklist.description: New MCP tool handlers are standalone exported async functions (handlePacksPreview, handlePacksCreate) that accept (args, deps, context) — matching the pattern of handlePacksGet, handlePacksList, etc.
checklist.example: export async function handlePacksPreview(args: PackPreviewInput, deps: McpDeps, context: HandlerContext): Promise<CallToolResult> { ... }
checklist.name: mcp_handler_follows_existing_pattern

checklist.description: Both new REST endpoints are registered inside the packRoutes function with correct HTTP method (POST), paths (/diaries/:id/packs/preview and /diaries/:id/packs), operationId, tags, security, and response schemas.
checklist.example: server.post('/diaries/:id/packs/preview', { schema: { operationId: 'previewDiaryCustomPack', tags: ['diary'], security: [{bearerAuth:[]}], ... } }, handler)
checklist.name: rest_endpoints_registered_correctly

checklist.description: packs_preview and packs_create MCP tools are registered via fastify.mcpAddTool() with name, description, and inputSchema.
checklist.example: fastify.mcpAddTool({ name: 'packs_preview', description: '...', inputSchema: PackPreviewSchema }, async (args, ctx) => handlePacksPreview(args, deps, ctx))
checklist.name: mcp_tools_registered

checklist.description: New response schemas (e.g. CustomPackResultSchema, CompileStatsSchema) are added to the sharedSchemas array so they are available for $ref resolution in the OpenAPI spec.
checklist.example: CompileStatsSchema, CustomPackEntryResultSchema, CustomPackResultSchema added to the sharedSchemas export in apps/rest-api/src/schemas.ts.
checklist.name: custom_pack_schemas_registered

checklist.description: After persisting the pack, the create endpoint calls the relationship writer to establish the pack-parent relation so permission checks work for the new pack.
checklist.example: await fastify.relationshipWriter.grantPackParent(pack.id, diary.id) is called after the transaction commits.
checklist.name: create_wires_authorization_graph

checklist.description: Pinned packs have no expiry date; unpinned packs receive an expiry date (e.g. 7 days from creation). The pinned field defaults to false when not provided.
checklist.example: const pinned = request.body.pinned ?? false; const expiresAt = pinned ? null : new Date(createdAtDate.getTime() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000);
checklist.name: pinned_vs_expiry_behavior

checklist.description: An entry that has no contentHash (cannot be packed into a content-addressed pack) is rejected with a validation error rather than silently ignored or causing a runtime crash.
checklist.example: loadSelectedEntries() checks row.contentHash and throws createProblem('validation-failed', ...) for entries with null contentHash.
checklist.name: entry_missing_content_hash_rejected

checklist.description: The implementation verifies that the diary belongs to the authenticated user before allowing pack creation or preview — a user cannot create a pack for another user's diary.
checklist.example: fastify.diaryService.findDiary(request.params.id, request.authContext.identityId) is called before processing entries; DiaryServiceError is translated to a 403/404 problem.
checklist.name: no_diary_ownership_bypass

context: Evaluate adding custom pack preview and create endpoints to the REST API and MCP server, allowing callers to build context packs from an explicit entry selection.
